reviews.review_description: My favourite part was looking outside the window in the painting with the girl. The portal behind, the simulation past the painting, the sounds are also cool. Some trees on the periphery are a bit repetitive. Overall, I would recommend this to friends. Make it longer pls.

Wow, quelle application ! Merci beaucoup pour ce moment féerique. Mona Lisa et la Création d'Adam, les détails sont spectaculaires. What a peaceful and wonderful moment—being inside the painting. The details of the Mona Lisa are speechless, and the Création of Adam—I literally spent 10 minutes just admiring it. Thank you for this special experience. We are looking forward to other creations, like Girl with a Pearl Earring, Vermeer, and Rembrandt. Please consider them.
I absolutely love this experience. I hope they make more. If they're reading this, I would love to see Nighthawks by Ed Hopper!
This is absolutely stunning and very relaxing I really wish there was more.  Really recommend it!🫶🏽
This is visually stunning. You literally get to walk inside paintings. It’s very peaceful, beautiful graphics. I only wish there were more. Definitely worth experiencing.

Amazing! Download it...
Pros: Art, art that surrounds you, high quality clear images of famous & maybe not so famous paintings but still really mind blowing. Entering the world of a painting sends you into a different dimension. Its like really being there... im trippin out! Honestly cant wait for more! 5* but 6* if you keep adding more paintings! Very well done devs! 💯

Very cool effect taking you inside the paintings
!!!

Just like a demo but is something you can show friends for their first time in vr, very simple controls. I use the quest optimizer and you can boost the graphics to 250%. 
I wish there was some dlc as I would buy more paintings.

Please, Martin and Martin (the developers), continue developing this wonderful experience. The concept of being inside a painting in VR isn't new, but the execution by this app is truly AAA quality. Two stand out: being inside Vermeer's Girl Reading a Letter at an Open Window is like stepping back to Delft in the 1600s, you can look out the grimy windows and see the ships, ripples on the sea inlet, and the seagulls swooping as the young girl reads the letter from her lover. Wonderful. Better yet is to sit inside Starry Night, looking down on the little French town, with yellow lights in the houses, as the amazing sky swirls. But Martin and Martin, why did you stop at 5 paintings? I'd happily pay 5x the price for 5x the paintings, especially those with a real sense of ancient place. Or an impressionist or two...? Could you do a Monet? Please...

I wanted to give my mom a new experience on Mother's Day, and this is the perfect app. There's no demanding mechanics or anything, you just point and click to enter the painting, then click again to get out of it.

Really, it's the perfect experience for an elder who isn't a gamer. My mom who usually doesn't "get" digital experiences really appreciated it.

reviews.review_title: It's actually worth the five stars
reviews.review_description: Honestly, for £1.49, I truly cannot complain. It's five classic paintings that you point at and click a button to basically get sucked directly into, so you're standing there inside them created as fully 3D immersive versions of the 2D paintings, and they actually really do look and sound great. And, while utterly simple and limited to basically this and nothing more, it's all very solid and highly polished. I'm hoping they add some more images in updates down the line, because I really enjoyed the set that's there and would like to see more.
Amazing experience

reviews.review_title: An essential app for art lovers
reviews.review_description: I got goosebumps from all the arts. Simply incredible. An essential app for art lovers. Looking forward to more additions. Please don't stop producing. It's worth every penny.

reviews.review_title: ART PLUNGE IS THE NEW STANDARD IN VR !!
reviews.review_description:
“ LIKE A RELIGIOUS EXPERIENCE , I FELT LIKE I MET GOD !! “ .
 “ THE FUTURE OF ART IS IN THE HERE AND NOW  “ . 
 “ ART PLUNGE IS LIGHTING THE PATH TO A BRIGHTER TOMORROW ! “ . 
 “ NOT JUST AN EXPERIENCE … , BUT A REVELATION . “ 
 “ A REVOLUTION IN ART BEGINS HERE !!! “ .
 “ IF YOU LOVE ART YOU GOTTA SEE THIS !! “ .
 “ ALL ART DESERVES TO BE “ ART PLUNGED !!! “ “ . 
 “ THE WAY I FEEL ART IN MY HEART AND SOUL … , MIND AND  BODY … , FOR THE WORLD TO LOVINGLY EMBRACE ! “ .
 “ LIKE  A FIRST KISS … , ART PLUNGE IS LIKE COMING HOME  TO YOUR  SOULMATE  … , AT LONG LAST  … “ .

 “ MARVELOUS  … , ABSOLUTELY  MARVELOUS  !! “ .

reviews.review_title: Downgrade Oculus GO version in some ways
reviews.review_description:
When using the Quest 1 & 2 and downloading the GO version to those headsets, which should still be a thing if you owned the software, it was so much better. You could get so much closer to the artwork, but now when you move just a little closer to the art from where you’re standing at, a grid appears around the subjects ruining the experience and immersion. You could walk all the way up to the subjects before and it was very cool. 

On top of all of this, it sucks they are charging for it again if you owned it before, and even with the slightly updated visuals, it’s the same content but with less immersion with a more immersive headset. 

At least it’s only $2, but I’m considering returning it because it’s a downgraded experience.

I’m disappointed to say the least as a fan of the GO version.
It's amazing, please next paintings

reviews.review_title: I would like to see more paintings adde
reviews.review_description: I would like more paintings to be added as downloadable content. If that happens, I would gladly pay to see them.
That's the app I show my friends first when they are new to VR!! I

I remember playing with this app in the Gear VR and i was mind blown even with the hardware limitations of that headset. This version is slightly better but I think its not taking advantage of room-scale VR and better resolution. Still I think is one of the best showcases of VR I've ever experienced. Great job, people.

Art Plunge is a short but fun distraction that takes five extremely well known paintings and transforms them into something different by adding a third dimension and extrapolating beyond the frame. Some might call it as corruption of great art, but others could see it as taking a new perspective on several over exposed pieces of art.

You arrive at this experience in a dark lobby, on one side a wall with five paintings and on the other blue marble earth on a starfield. Each of the five paintings presented in their original aspect with a brief accompanying text, and an invitation to "enter into our VR interpretation". Plunging into the paining, the frame dissolves and the image takes on a new fully realized depth (no flat 3D layers here) and the periphery surrounds you with new detail complimentary to the original. Ambient audio nicely adds to the level of immersion.

The five paintings are Vermeer's Girl Reading a Letter at an Open Window, Botticelli's The Birth of Venus, DaVinci's Mona Lisa, Van Gogh's Starry Night and Michalengelo's The Creation Of Adam. These VR interpretations certainly distort the original intent of the artists, but only the stuffiest of art historians are likely to not get something out of this fun experience.

If there was anything we could recommend to improve this experience, it would be for an opportunity to view the original painting in some detail. Also the artworks' descriptions are very brief - there would be value in a lengthier description about the history and interpretation of each painting, and perhaps also further text explaining the VR re-interpretation.

✅ Fun re-imaginings add new insights into overexposed art
✅ Does something only possible in VR
❌ Short experience

reviews.review_title: One of the best VR showcases
reviews.review_description: This demo shows the potential of VR for art and education. By the time of this review, there are only 5 paintings available to "plunge" in, but I'm hoping they will expand the gallery by the time if goes to the regular store.
Who doesn't want to walk into a great work of art? My first reaction was "Wow." Please add more art.  I would love some Millais or Waterhouse.
Well done and thank you. This is really impressive work.
Please... more art.  We are all happy to pay money for each new painting you add!  I'd pay $10 per painting!
Stunning. We beg for more!
The best two dollars I've spent in a long time!  Get this one, you won't be sorry.

What a neat idea. Really enjoyed this! Stepping inside the paintings - seeing the people blinking, the wind blowing, etc. I would love more paintings added to the gallery. :) Well worth the couple bucks.

reviews.review_title: Experience art in a whole new way
reviews.review_description:
The idea/concept is simply ingenious. How would the scene shown in the painting look to us if we were in the middle of it? Great realisation.... Of course, the perspective is still frontal, even if it is three-dimensional, so it is logical that the freedom of movement is restricted..... by the way, I also find the "holodeck" effect very successful when we move out of the area.

Great experience that will hopefully be extended, but definitely worth the price!

A few comments / change requests:
- "starry night" - a little more atmospheric noises (in the other paintings this is more coherent/immersive).
- make the "wormhole" less scary.... perhaps as a white vortex. It looks really spooky when you turn around for the first time. Help!
- expansion through payable DLC in "package format" with 2-3 paintings in which 1-2 are very well known, but in which less well known but still no less interesting paintings are also included to increase the interest in paintings overall
- please add works by Dali, Gaudi, Hunderwasser and Royo...


Thx for that great experience of art :-)
Nice idea, but too little content.

Very interesting and good experience. Waiting for update.

Ps: my mom claims about the "girl at the window" reflex, that doesn't move accordingly with the subject 🙂 But apart from this, she is enthusiastic as well about this app.

Ok so like there is only five paintings or something to go in and once you have done them that is it the entire thing is over in like 10 minutes, usually I would request a refund but the graphics are so nice the game itself is a work of art and so I will not request a refund, and will return to the game if new paintings are added, worth it for the money, very nice

In one word: wonderful! 
Please insert other artworks. For example something by Escher. 
I'm waiting for new paintings...
Compliments for this app. Good!
This is an awesome experience! It just needs way more paintings.
Really amazing work
More more more!

reviews.review_title: A downgrade compared to Go version
reviews.review_description:
1 star because of below frustrating reason only, otherwise the concept is great and price tag more than honest.

Resolution may be higher (?), but your 6DoF moves are restricted to a very narrow area, beyond which visuals are forced to black & white polygons…
I perfectly understand that it’s because the extrapolation of the paintings is limited to said area perspective and thus wouldn’t sustainable enough to watch beyond these limits, but I don’t care: please DO NOT LIMIT 6DOF, because it is one of Quest main feature vs Gear VR and Go.

Since there seems to be absolutely no improvement or additions to this Quest version compared to the Go version which still runs on Quest 1 without any 6DOF limited area, this Quest version is purely useless and I will therefore ask for a refund.

This is a exiting new way to enjoy old masterpieces in detail never seen before. Very immersive with the matching sound scapes. The only downside is that there is not much content at time of review (August 2021).
I really hope that the developers will add new living paintings to the gallery.
In summary, it is really worth to check this experience out.

It is amazing how wonderful this title is. Amazing work. What I cannot understand is why the developers do not continue working on this product, adding paints or other options. Congratulations but please add material.

To say that classic works of art are brought
to life here would be an understatement. The concept is inspired, the attention to detail is astounding, and the result is profound. This is a new dimension of appreciation; an expansion and enrichment of both context and content, but one that is deeply respectful of the original work.

5 stars just aren't enough to rate this experience.
This app is worth more than what they are asking for.  I enjoyed very much but also this app is excellent app to show off for friends and family.
VR Technology put to excellent use.  I really hope the developer continues to add paintings,I’d especially like to see Jan van Eyck’s Last Judgement…
I used to own this on the go and it would always be the first thing i would show people to introduce them to VR! So happy i can finally enjoy it on the Quest!
Loved all of them, especially the birth of Venus. Please release more! I’d be willing to pay for packs.

If you have any interest in art or just want an impressive showcase of what VR is capable of, get Art Plunge. 
This was one of my favourite apps on Oculus Go and I must say it only got better on Quest 2. It's now even higher res and in 6dof.

Also, I have no idea why it's on App Lab and not in the official Quest store.

reviews.review_title: My best experience in VR by far!
reviews.review_description: This is so beautiful and deep. You are yourselves great artists. Please do more.

there's something strange, all the reviews have disappeared. it tells me about an update but I see that the version is still the old one and there are no development notes. I don't trust myself to update....

It's obvious they put a lot of work into this. amazing quality, extremely well made, impeccable detail. Well worth the price of admission. Please make more, I'll buy them all. Even Goya's Black Paintings.

reviews.review_title: Good idea. But just a few "rooms"..
reviews.review_description: I love idea of that experience. What i don't like that  environment is bit cheap and it is pretty "short". Let's update it and add more ARTs please :)
I fall in love with the game immediately at the first painting! I enjoy spending time in every piece of the magnificent world. Please make more experience for us~
